title: POST /api/admin/test-emails — operator deliverability + voice check
problem: The portal sends ~12 distinct email types (magic link, status changes, message notifications, payment confirmations, digest, etc.). Each has its own template, voice, lang variant. Without a fire-everything diagnostic, the operator only sees an email type by actually walking the matching user flow — slow, hard to QA changes, easy to miss a regression. /api/admin/test-emails fires one of every type at the signed-in admin's address with synthetic sample data. Zero production rows read or touched.
analysis: POST only. Operator gate (currentEmail + isAdmin). CSRF enforced by middleware. Iterates a defined list of email types, calls each one with synthetic sample data hardcoded in the handler. Pacing: ~600ms sleep between sends to stay under Resend's 2 req/s free-tier rate. Total: 12 sends × 600ms = ~7s, well under Pages Functions' 30s wall-time budget. Returns 200 { sent: [...types] } on success.

decisions.decision: Sequential pacing instead of Promise.all, stay under Resend's 2 req/s
decisions.rationale: Resend's free tier rate-limits at ~2 req/s. Promise.all would burst all 12 at once and hit the limit, getting 429s on the latter half. Sequential with ~600ms gap pacing keeps every send under the rate limit + gets all 12 in under 10 seconds.
decisions.recommendation: Don't refactor to parallel. If Resend's rate ever increases, evaluate; until then, sequential is correct.

decisions.decision: Synthetic sample data, never read prod rows
decisions.rationale: Reading real visitor data to test an email template would leak that data into the test email — even to the operator, that's a privacy footnote. Hardcoded synthetic data ('Test Visitor', 'sample@example.com', a fixture intake JSON) makes the test deterministic + privacy-safe.
decisions.recommendation: Don't make the sample data dynamic. If a future need wants 'render this email type for THIS real session', that's a different endpoint with different gating.

successCriteria:
- Operator gets ~12 emails (one per type) at their address
- Each email uses synthetic data; no prod rows read
- Sequential pacing stays under Resend rate limit
- Total send completes well under 30s wall-time
- 200 + sent list on success
implementation: functions/api/admin/test-emails.ts — onRequestPost. EMAIL_TYPES array drives the iteration. Sleep helper between sends. Tests: functions/api/admin/test-emails.test.ts covers auth + the type iteration shape.
userGuide: Operator only. POST /api/admin/test-emails (with CSRF token). Wait ~10s; check inbox. Use to: verify deliverability after Resend config changes, eyeball a new email template, regression-check voice across the catalog. No UI link by default; access via curl or browser devtools.
knownLimitations: Sends to the signed-in admin's address only — can't target another address (avoids accidental spam vector)
Synthetic data is hardcoded; a new email type requires updating both EMAIL_TYPES and possibly the sample fixture
If Resend hits a temporary outage during the send loop, latter emails fail — handler returns 200 with the partial sent list (best-effort)
No dry-run mode; sending IS the test

statusHistory.reason: Endpoint shipped with all 12 email types + rate-paced sequential sends.
Code-first doc co-located. Synthetic-data + pacing decisions stable.
